jokes.text: Rehearsal? We did a show last week. I just came to pick up a paycheck.
jokes.setup: Tracy being called to rehearsal for the weekly show
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding of how television works
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment - his celebrity entitlement and disconnect from reality. Well-structured with clear setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Next Friday is Damon Dash's birthday party. And the Friday after that there's an orgy over at Elizabeth Hasselbeck's.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he can't do future shows
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd celebrity obligations, ending with conservative TV host orgy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and juxtaposition - Elizabeth Hasselbeck hosting an orgy is perfectly absurd. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: I'm an improviser. My acting style is like jazz. Jazz that you laugh at.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his artistic approach with pretentious jazz comparison
jokes.punchline: The deflating qualifier that undermines his pretension
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - starts pretentious, ends self-aware. Great character integration showing Tracy's mix of ego and self-knowledge.

jokes.text: It's a dance-pop techno hybrid called Muffin Top. I gave everybody copies at Christmas?
jokes.setup: Jenna mentioning her song to Pete
jokes.punchline: The terrible title and the fact she forced it on everyone as Christmas gifts
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Jenna's desperation and tone-deafness. The title 'Muffin Top' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Oh...no.
jokes.setup: Pete being asked if he remembers Jenna's Christmas song
jokes.punchline: His honest, deflated reaction revealing he clearly remembers and regrets it
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - his pained response tells the whole story about how terrible the song was.

jokes.text: Well, apparently it's a number-one hit in Israel.
jokes.setup: Jenna's song being discussed
jokes.punchline: The random, specific success in Israel
jokes.explanation: Absurdist specificity works well - why Israel? The randomness adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: You should know that Muffin Top is also number four in Belgium.
jokes.setup: Already established the Israel success
jokes.punchline: Adding another random European country to inflate her importance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of her delusion. The specificity of 'number four' makes it funnier than just saying it's popular.

jokes.text: And I'm here to talk to you today about a wonderful new synergy. It's called 'product integration.'
jokes.setup: Jack presenting to the writers about corporate synergy
jokes.punchline: The show literally doing what he's describing - meta commentary on product placement
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy - the show is doing exactly what Jack is pitching. Smart and self-aware writing.

jokes.text: positive mentions-- or PosMens--of GE products
jokes.setup: Explaining product integration strategy
jokes.punchline: Creating the ridiculous abbreviation 'PosMens'
jokes.explanation: Excellent corporate satire - 'PosMens' sounds both corporate and ridiculous

jokes.text: For example, you could write an episode where one of your characters purchases-- and is satisfied with... one of GE's direct-current drilling motors for an offshore or land-based project.
jokes.setup: Jack giving an example of product integration
jokes.punchline: The completely absurd suggestion of drilling motor product placement on a comedy show
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdism - the idea of comedy characters needing drilling equipment is wonderfully ridiculous.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. You're saying you want us to use the show to sell stuff?
jokes.setup: Liz responding to Jack's product integration pitch
jokes.punchline: Her naive shock at something that's obviously already happening in television
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Liz's artistic idealism versus reality. Sets up the Snapple sequence.

jokes.text: Wow. This is diet Snapple? I know, it tastes just like regular Snapple, doesn't it? You should try Plum-A-Granate. It's amazing. I only date guys who drink Snapple.
jokes.setup: Liz just protested against selling products on the show
jokes.punchline: Immediate product placement happening organically while she's speaking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - shows how product placement already happens naturally. Perfect timing and execution.

jokes.text: Get real, kids. You write skits mocking our presidents to fill time between car commercials.
jokes.setup: Writers claiming they're artists
jokes.punchline: Jack's brutal reality check about what television actually is
jokes.explanation: Sharp, cynical truth about television that's both funny and cutting. Great character voice for Jack.

jokes.text: Josh gets a lot of fan mail for Gaybraham Lincoln.
jokes.setup: Defending the show's artistic merit
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous character name that combines historical figure with gay stereotype
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that establishes the show-within-a-show's lowbrow humor. Very quotable portmanteau.

jokes.text: Did you guys know that Holland is the only country with a national dog?
jokes.setup: Serious discussion about product integration
jokes.punchline: Complete topic change to random trivia from Snapple cap
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the Snapple joke - shows how the product placement has completely derailed the meeting.

jokes.text: I'm starting to think... he can't read!
jokes.setup: Discussion about Tracy not following cue cards
jokes.punchline: Jenna's dramatic conclusion jumping to illiteracy
jokes.explanation: Sets up major plot thread. Good character moment for Jenna's dramatic tendencies.

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Tracy Jordan. I'm black, NBC! Very proud, like peacocks, right, Janet?
jokes.setup: Liz claiming Tracy reads promos
jokes.punchline: His completely off-script, stream-of-consciousness promo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy voice - the random NBC/peacock connection and calling someone Janet. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: It says, 'Emergency exit only'. He couldn't read that?
jokes.setup: Tracy leaving through wrong door
jokes.punchline: More evidence supporting the illiteracy theory
jokes.explanation: Good plot development but not particularly funny on its own. Functional comedy.

jokes.text: What's really sad is that while she's in this fragile state, none of her friends are messing with her head.
jokes.setup: Frank pretending to be sympathetic about Jenna
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests they should mess with her vulnerable state
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - fake sympathy immediately undercut by cruel intentions

jokes.text: My friend in accounting, Lando Calrissian, he says Jack's probably gonna have to fire an actor.
jokes.setup: Frank sharing inside information
jokes.punchline: His accountant friend having the name of a Star Wars character
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist naming convention. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's like shooting fish in a barrel. Yeah, if the fish have daddy issues.
jokes.setup: Frank commenting on manipulating Jenna
jokes.punchline: Toofer's psychological insight about why she's so easy to manipulate
jokes.explanation: Great twist on a common saying. Psychologically astute and character-specific for both speakers.

jokes.text: Let's go rescue Karen or whatever. Is there a Cheryl? Then she could tell us where drugs are-- I mean gold-- Then we got the car chase. I'm getting way too old for this!
jokes.setup: Discussing Tracy's movie scripts
jokes.punchline: Generic action movie tropes delivered stream-of-consciousness style
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of bad action movies. The slip from 'drugs' to 'gold' is particularly good.

jokes.text: We spend all this money in Iraq, but meanwhile, our inner-city graduation rates are lower than they are in the Sudan.
jokes.setup: Liz getting socially conscious about education
jokes.punchline: Her completely made-up and absurd statistic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's tendency toward uninformed liberal talking points.

jokes.text: That doesn't sound right. Maybe it was Sweden. Maybe it was teen pregnancy.
jokes.setup: Her previous false statistic
jokes.punchline: Her complete uncertainty about her own 'facts'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how her social consciousness is all surface-level. The uncertainty makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I gotta read more.
jokes.setup: Her admission of ignorance
jokes.punchline: The understated resolution that highlights how uninformed she really is
jokes.explanation: Nice button on the sequence. Economical and character-appropriate conclusion.

jokes.text: Hippy humor. That's what I'm going for.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining the self-referential comedy approach
jokes.punchline: Jack's dismissive corporate categorization of counterculture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack voice - reducing anti-establishment comedy to a corporate buzzword.

jokes.text: I'm watching Friends right now. What happens with Ross and Rachel? No, no. Don't tell me. Seriously. What were you saying?
jokes.setup: Jack claiming to study comedy
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to not ask for spoilers while claiming he doesn't want them
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's controlling nature and impatience. The contradiction is well-executed.

jokes.text: You're such a Monica! Ah ha! You are.
jokes.setup: Liz being controlling about rehearsal details
jokes.punchline: Jack applying his Friends research to categorize Liz
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to his Friends studying. Shows Jack trying to apply his comedy research practically.

jokes.text: Uh, when has it not worked? It'll be a 45-minute wait. Oh, will it?
jokes.setup: Jenna defending her sexuality as a weapon
jokes.punchline: Her demonstration with the hostess falling completely flat
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment. Shows Jenna's delusion about her seductive powers.

jokes.text: Yeah, so now you just have to make yourself ten years younger and Asian.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming to know what Jack likes
jokes.punchline: Liz's brutal assessment of Jack's romantic preferences
jokes.explanation: Sharp character insight delivered with perfect economy. Dark but accurate assessment.

jokes.text: Can I read? Please don't get angry. It's not your fault. It's the system. Did you ever see Hoop Dreams? It's like that.
jokes.setup: Liz delicately approaching the literacy question
jokes.punchline: Her overly careful, condescending approach treating Tracy like a victim
jokes.explanation: Perfect white liberal guilt moment. Liz's condescension is cringey but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I can't read, Liz Lemon! My shameful secret is out. Now you know why I'm always running into the ladies' bathroom.
jokes.setup: Tracy confessing to illiteracy
jokes.punchline: The bathroom mix-ups explained by inability to read signs
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and explanation for previous character behavior. Tracy's dramatic delivery works well.

jokes.text: I sign my name with an X! I once tried to make mashed potatoes with laundry detergent!
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing his illiteracy confession
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd consequences of not being able to read
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with vivid, absurd examples. The laundry detergent detail is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: I think I voted for Nader! Nader!
jokes.setup: Continuing the illiteracy consequences
jokes.punchline: The ultimate horror - accidentally voting for Ralph Nader
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation climax. The repetition of 'Nader!' shows his horror. Topical and character-specific.

jokes.text: All thanks to one very, very special white lady.
jokes.setup: Tracy thanking Liz for helping him
jokes.punchline: The patronizing phrase 'special white lady' highlighting the racial dynamic
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered through character voice. Highlights the racial undertones perfectly.

jokes.text: Damn, George Will just gets more and more conservative.
jokes.setup: Liz reading while Tracy's gone
jokes.punchline: Her liberal shock at a notoriously conservative pundit being conservative
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's political naivety. Topical reference that fits character.

jokes.text: Jack in the GE promo video failing repeatedly
jokes.setup: Jack attempting to film a simple corporate video
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to perform despite his corporate confidence
jokes.explanation: Extended sequence showing Jack's vulnerability. Great character development through sustained cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Is it this? Or, if I may, this?
jokes.setup: Jack struggling with what to do with his arms
jokes.punchline: His formal corporate language while discussing basic human movement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jack applying corporate formality to physical comedy. 'If I may' is perfectly pompous.

jokes.text: It's called, 'racial integration'-- No, that's not right, is it? It's called, 'product intergort'-- 'intergortian'?
jokes.setup: Jack trying to say 'product integration'
jokes.punchline: His complete meltdown including accidentally saying 'racial integration'
jokes.explanation: Great malapropism sequence. The racial integration slip is particularly cringey and funny.

jokes.text: Ha! That was fun, you guys. Didn't feel like five days, did it?
jokes.setup: Jack finishing the disastrous video shoot
jokes.punchline: His delusional positive spin on obvious failure, revealing it took five days
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jack's inability to admit failure and the revelation that it took five days to shoot a simple promo.

jokes.text: I've summited Kilimanjaro. I've showered with Greta Van Susteren.
jokes.setup: Jack listing his accomplishments to show he likes challenges
jokes.punchline: The bizarre and uncomfortable second example after the impressive first one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - goes from impressive to deeply uncomfortable. The specificity of Greta Van Susteren is perfect.

jokes.text: ...correlate overseas earnings report dynamics?
jokes.setup: Liz asking about Jack's meeting with his boss
jokes.punchline: Her attempt at corporate speak that sounds completely made up
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz trying to sound corporate and failing. The buzzword salad is appropriately meaningless.

jokes.text: Actions speak louder than words. Take care of it. Don't worry, I'll have it on your desk first thing in the morning. Look, I am tired of your promises! I want this thing fixed.
jokes.setup: Jack apparently being berated by his boss
jokes.punchline: Later revealed to be rehearsing with an extra who's complaining about health insurance
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection setup that pays off later. Creates false drama that gets undercut beautifully.

jokes.text: I take it? Uh... I--I take it, uh, with the non-dairy creamer. Mmm. Yeah. Non-dairy creamer. Yum.
jokes.setup: Jenna flirting with who she thinks is Jack's boss
jokes.punchline: The extra's awkward, confused response to her seduction attempt
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Jenna's seduction attempt falls flat with confused extra.

jokes.text: Those cards are a little confusing.
jokes.setup: Jack making mistakes during rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Blaming the cue cards instead of his performance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's inability to accept blame

jokes.text: I think I can do it. No. You should definitely do it. It'll be hilarious. Oh, well, that'd be a refreshing change of pace for the show, wouldn't it?
jokes.setup: Liz trying to get Jack out of performing
jokes.punchline: Jack's cutting insult about the show's usual quality
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment - Jack's ego and condescension coming through even when he's struggling.

jokes.text: To get out of coming to work. Oh, so first you thought he was illiterate, and now you think he's lazy? Jeez, you are racist.
jokes.setup: Liz figuring out Tracy's deception
jokes.punchline: Pete pointing out the progression of her assumptions about Tracy
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary on unconscious bias. The escalation of accusations is well-constructed.

jokes.text: No. Tracy took advantage of my white guilt, which is to be used only for good, like over-tipping and supporting Barack Obama.
jokes.setup: Liz defending herself against racism charge
jokes.punchline: Her specific and revealing examples of 'good' white guilt applications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Liz's self-aware but still problematic approach to race. The specificity makes it funnier and more real.

jokes.text: You hear this mess about sometimes Y being a vowel? What a world.
jokes.setup: Tracy pretending to come from reading class
jokes.punchline: His world-weary reaction to basic grammar rules
jokes.explanation: Great continuation of the illiteracy performance. His philosophical reaction to Y as vowel is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I bow-hunt polar bear. I once drove a rental car into the Hudson just to practice escaping.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining he's not afraid of dangerous situations
jokes.punchline: Increasingly extreme and unnecessary examples of his fearlessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moments - the polar bear hunting and rental car destruction are wonderfully extreme and specific.

jokes.text: Ten years ago, I was an inch-and-a-half shorter than I am today. Sheer willpower.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his determination to accomplish goals
jokes.punchline: The impossible claim that willpower made him grow as an adult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment that's perfectly in character. Jack's belief in corporate willpower extending to physical impossibilities.

jokes.text: Josh can do this, and earlier today, he ate a club sandwich with the toothpicks still in it. Jenna can do this, and she was once engaged to David Blaine.
jokes.setup: Liz giving Jack a pep talk about acting ability
jokes.punchline: Her examples of how stupid his co-workers are, with absurd supporting evidence
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating insults. The toothpick detail and David Blaine engagement are perfectly absurd and specific.

jokes.text: If you were any other woman on earth... I would be turned on right now.
jokes.setup: Liz giving Jack an aggressive pep talk
jokes.punchline: Jack's backhanded compliment about her dominance being attractive but not on her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack line - simultaneously complimentary and insulting. Great character dynamic with Liz.

jokes.text: That guy's an extra on the show. No. He said he was Jack's boss. No. In the sketch. He doesn't even have a line.
jokes.setup: Jenna bragging about hooking up with Jack's boss
jokes.punchline: The revelation that 'Ron' is just an extra in the sketch
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to earlier misdirection. Jenna's delusion meeting reality is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: What is the point of belonging to the Extras Guild if I cannot get health coverage for my foot, hmm?
jokes.setup: Revealing what 'Ron' was actually complaining about to Jack
jokes.punchline: His mundane union grievance about foot health coverage
jokes.explanation: Good callback that explains the earlier scene. The specificity of foot coverage is absurd and perfect.

jokes.text: Do you have any left?
jokes.setup: Jenna threatening to use her sexuality for revenge
jokes.punchline: Liz's brutal question about whether Jenna has any sexuality remaining
jokes.explanation: Brutal and economical insult. Perfect timing and delivery from Liz's character.

jokes.text: Where the hot lesbians at, Lemon? I knew it! You can read!
jokes.setup: Tracy arriving and asking about lesbians
jokes.punchline: Liz's realization that he read the call sheet to know about the lesbian scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect trap and reveal. Tracy's first line immediately exposes his literacy to attentive viewers and characters.

jokes.text: I even have a column in Ebony called Musings!
jokes.setup: Tracy admitting he can read
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he's actually an intellectual with a magazine column
jokes.explanation: Great character reveal and escalation. 'Musings' as a column title is perfectly pretentious.

jokes.text: That's the subtle racism of lowered expectations. Bing Crosby said that. No, Bill Cosby said that. That's racist.
jokes.setup: Tracy calling out Liz's assumptions
jokes.punchline: The Bing/Bill Crosby confusion creating another racist moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Liz makes one mistake while trying to be enlightened, immediately makes another. The Crosby confusion is perfect.

jokes.text: I once shot a whole movie without ever getting out of my car? Yeah, I paid to see that. That was supposed to be a western.
jokes.setup: Tracy bragging about his lazy filmmaking
jokes.punchline: Liz revealing she actually saw this terrible movie and its genre
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - a western shot entirely from a car. Liz actually paying to see it makes it funnier.

jokes.text: When Gaybraham Lincoln gets hit in the crotch, can I go cross-eyed? Oh, yes, do that. That's hilarious!
jokes.setup: Tracy asking about performance choices
jokes.punchline: The combination of the earlier Gaybraham Lincoln reference with lowbrow physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Gaybraham Lincoln. Shows the lowbrow nature of the show-within-a-show.

jokes.text: Which of these is less offensive? Ahh! Ahh! Ahhh! Or... Ooh-hunh! Unh-hunh! Ohhh!
jokes.setup: Jenna asking about censorship of her moaning
jokes.punchline: Her performing increasingly sexual sounds for comparison
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Jenna's complete lack of self-awareness about how inappropriate she's being.

jokes.text: Lemon, these pages are blank.
jokes.setup: Liz giving Jack new script pages
jokes.punchline: The revelation that they're sabotaging him with blank pages
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the live show disaster. Economical revelation that explains what will happen.

jokes.text: You figured out Frank and I lied to you, so to get even, you're trying to get me naked on the roof and leave me there.
jokes.setup: Jenna's seduction attempt
jokes.punchline: Toofer immediately seeing through her scheme and its specific details
jokes.explanation: Good counter-scheme moment. Toofer's intelligence cutting through Jenna's manipulation.

jokes.text: No, you're all fired.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if they're cool with Jack
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact announcement of firing everyone
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment - his corporate ruthlessness delivered casually. The economy makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Don't be cute, Lemon. You're too old for that.
jokes.setup: Liz being playful about watching Jack's performance
jokes.punchline: Jack's brutal age-related insult
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment. Jack's casual cruelty delivered with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Does she know we went off the air two minutes ago? No, she does not. Smart move.
jokes.setup: Jenna performing Muffin Top enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: The revelation that the show ended but no one wants to stop her
jokes.explanation: Good ending moment. Shows everyone's relief at Jenna being happy and occupied.
